overview: A minimal single-page web application that serves a static 'Hello World' message to visitors. The architecture consists of a single frontend component built with React and TypeScript that renders the greeting message when accessed through a browser. No backend services are required as the application serves purely static content.
requirements: The application must display 'Hello World' text when a visitor accesses it in a web browser
The application must be accessible via HTTP/HTTPS
The application must respond with a valid HTML page containing the greeting
The application must include a health check endpoint for monitoring

components.name: hello-world-web
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Hello World Web Application
  version: 1.0.0
  description: A simple web application that displays Hello World
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check endpoint
      description: Returns the health status of the web application
      operationId: healthCheck
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Application is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
                  timestamp:
                    type: string
                    format: date-time
                    example: '2024-01-15T10:30:00Z'
components.componentAgentInstructions:
Implement a single-page React application using TypeScript and Vite as the build tool. The application should:

1. Create a React component that renders 'Hello World' centered on the page with appropriate styling
2. Use modern React functional components with hooks
3. Include basic CSS styling to center the text both horizontally and vertically on the viewport
4. Set up a health check endpoint at /health that returns JSON with status 'ok' and current timestamp
5. Configure Vite to serve the application on port 3000
6. Create a production-optimized Docker image using a multi-stage build:
   - Stage 1: Build the React app using node:18-alpine with npm install and npm run build
   - Stage 2: Serve static files using nginx:alpine, copying build output to /usr/share/nginx/html
7. Configure nginx to:
   - Serve the React app on port 80
   - Handle /health endpoint to return JSON response
   - Redirect all routes to index.html for SPA routing
8. Include error boundaries to handle any runtime errors gracefully
9. Add meta tags for proper HTML5 document structure
10. Ensure the application is fully responsive and works on mobile and desktop browsers
11. The Dockerfile should expose port 80 and use nginx as the final base image
12. Include .dockerignore to exclude node_modules and build artifacts from Docker context
